registers.name: CR1
registers.description: Power control register 1

fields.name: LPMS
fields.description: Low-power mode selection These bits select the low-power mode entered when CPU enters the Deepsleep mode. 1xx: Shutdown mode Note: If LPR bit is set, Stop 2 mode cannot be selected and Stop 1 mode shall be entered instead of Stop 2. Note: In Standby mode, SRAM2 can be preserved or not, depending on RRS bit configuration in PWR_CR3.

fields.name: RRSTP
fields.description: SRAM3 retention in Stop 2 mode

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: SRAM3 is powered off in Stop 2 mode (SRAM3 content is lost)

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: SRAM3 is powered in Stop 2 mode (RAM3 content is kept)

fields.name: DBP
fields.description: Disable backup domain write protection In reset state, the RTC and backup registers are protected against parasitic write access. This bit must be set to enable write access to these registers.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Access to RTC and Backup registers disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Access to RTC and Backup registers enabled

fields.name: VOS
fields.description: Voltage scaling range selection

fields.name: LPR
fields.description: Low-power run When this bit is set, the regulator is switched from main mode (MR) to low-power mode (LPR). Note: Stop 2 mode cannot be entered when LPR bit is set. Stop 1 is entered instead.

registers.name: CR2
registers.description: Power control register 2

fields.name: PVDE
fields.description: Power voltage detector enable Note: This bit is write-protected when the bit PVDL (PVD Lock) is set in the SYSCFG_CBR register. Note: This bit is reset only by a system reset.

fields.name: PLS
fields.description: Power voltage detector level selection. These bits select the voltage threshold detected by the power voltage detector: Note: These bits are write-protected when the bit PVDL (PVD Lock) is set in the SYSCFG_CBR register. Note: These bits are reset only by a system reset.

enum_values.name: PVDIN
enum_values.description: External input analog voltage PVD_IN (compared internally to VREFINT)

fields.name: PVME1
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring 1 enable: V<sub>DDUSB</sub> vs. 1.2V

fields.name: PVME2
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring 2 enable: V<sub>DDIO2</sub> vs. 0.9V

fields.name: PVME3
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring 3 enable: V<sub>DDA</sub> vs. 1.62V

fields.name: PVME4
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring 4 enable: V<sub>DDA</sub> vs. 2.2V

fields.name: IOSV
fields.description: V<sub>DDIO2</sub> Independent I/Os supply valid This bit is used to validate the V<sub>DDIO2</sub> supply for electrical and logical isolation purpose. Setting this bit is mandatory to use PG[15:2]. If V<sub>DDIO2</sub> is not always present in the application, the PVM can be used to determine whether this supply is ready or not.

enum_values.name: NotPresent
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 is not present. Logical and electrical isolation is applied to ignore this supply

fields.name: USV
fields.description: V<sub>DDUSB</sub> USB supply valid This bit is used to validate the V<sub>DDUSB</sub> supply for electrical and logical isolation purpose. Setting this bit is mandatory to use the USB OTG_FS peripheral. If V<sub>DDUSB</sub> is not always present in the application, the PVM can be used to determine whether this supply is ready or not.

enum_values.name: NotPresent
enum_values.description: VDDUSB is not present. Logical and electrical isolation is applied to ignore this supply

registers.name: CR3
registers.description: Power control register 3

fields.name: EWUP1
fields.description: Enable Wakeup pin WKUP1 When this bit is set, the external wakeup pin WKUP1 is enabled and triggers a wakeup from Standby or Shutdown event when a rising or a falling edge occurs. The active edge is configured via the WP1 bit in the PWR_CR4 register.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: External Wakeup pin WKUPx is disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: When this bit is set, the external wakeup pin WKUPx is enabled and triggers a wakeup from Standby or Shutdown event when a rising or a falling edge occurs. The active edge is configured via the WPx bit in the PWR_CR4 register

fields.name: EWUP2
fields.description: Enable Wakeup pin WKUP2 When this bit is set, the external wakeup pin WKUP2 is enabled and triggers a wakeup from Standby or Shutdown event when a rising or a falling edge occurs. The active edge is configured via the WP2 bit in the PWR_CR4 register.

fields.name: EWUP3
fields.description: Enable Wakeup pin WKUP3 When this bit is set, the external wakeup pin WKUP3 is enabled and triggers a wakeup from Standby or Shutdown event when a rising or a falling edge occurs. The active edge is configured via the WP3 bit in the PWR_CR4 register.

fields.name: EWUP4
fields.description: Enable Wakeup pin WKUP4 When this bit is set, the external wakeup pin WKUP4 is enabled and triggers a wakeup from Standby or Shutdown event when a rising or a falling edge occurs. The active edge is configured via the WP4 bit in the PWR_CR4 register.

fields.name: EWUP5
fields.description: Enable Wakeup pin WKUP5 When this bit is set, the external wakeup pin WKUP5 is enabled and triggers a wakeup from Standby or Shutdown event when a rising or a falling edge occurs.The active edge is configured via the WP5 bit in the PWR_CR4 register.

fields.name: RRS
fields.description: SRAM2 retention in Standby mode For STM32L4Rxxx and STM32L4Sxxx devices bit 9 is reserved For STM32L4P5xx and STM32L4Q5xx devices:

enum_values.name: PoweredOff
enum_values.description: SRAM2 is powered off in Standby mode (SRAM2 content is lost)

enum_values.name: PoweredOn
enum_values.description: Full SRAM2 is powered by the low-power regulator in Standby mode (SRAM2 full content is kept)

enum_values.name: PartialPoweredOn
enum_values.description: Only 4 Kbytes of SRAM2 is powered by the low-power regulator in Standby mode (4 Kbytes of SRAM2 content is kept)

fields.name: APC
fields.description: Apply pull-up and pull-down configuration When this bit is set, the I/O pull-up and pull-down configurations defined in the PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx registers are applied. When this bit is cleared, the PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx registers are not applied to the I/Os, instead the I/Os will be in floating mode during Standby or configured according GPIO controller GPIOx_PUPDR register during Run mode.

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: When this bit is set, the I/O pull-up and pull-down configurations defined in the PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx registers are applied. When this bit is cleared, the PWR_PUCRx and PWR_PDCRx registers are not applied to the I/Os, instead the I/Os will be in floating mode during Standby or configured according GPIO controller GPIOx_PUPDR register during Run mode

fields.name: ENULP
fields.description: Enable ULP sampling When this bit is set, the BORL, BORH and PVD are periodically sampled instead continuous monitoring to reduce power consumption. Fast supply drop between two sample/compare phases is not detected in this mode. This bit has impact only on STOP2, Standby and shutdown low power modes. Note: Available on STM32L4P5xx andSTM32L4Q5xx only.

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: When this bit is set, the BORL, BORH and PVD are periodically sampled instead continuous monitoring to reduce power consumption. Fast supply drop between two sample/compare phases is not detected in this mode. This bit has impact only on STOP2, Standby and shutdown low power modes

fields.name: DSIPDEN
fields.description: Enable Pull-down activation on DSI pins

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Pull-Down is disabled on DSI pins

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Pull-Down is enabled on DSI pins

fields.name: EIWUL
fields.description: Enable internal wakeup line

registers.name: CR4
registers.description: Power control register 4

fields.name: WP1
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP1 polarity This bit defines the polarity used for an event detection on external wake-up pin, WKUP1

fields.name: WP2
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP2 polarity This bit defines the polarity used for an event detection on external wake-up pin, WKUP2

fields.name: WP3
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP3 polarity This bit defines the polarity used for an event detection on external wake-up pin, WKUP3

fields.name: WP4
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP4 polarity This bit defines the polarity used for an event detection on external wake-up pin, WKUP4

fields.name: WP5
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP5 polarity This bit defines the polarity used for an event detection on external wake-up pin, WKUP5

fields.name: VBE
fields.description: V<sub>BAT</sub> battery charging enable

fields.name: VBRS
fields.description: V<sub>BAT</sub> battery charging resistor selection

enum_values.name: R5k
enum_values.description: Charge VBAT through a 5 kOhms resistor

enum_values.name: R1k5
enum_values.description: Charge VBAT through a 1.5 kOhms resistor

fields.name: EXT_SMPS_ON
fields.description: external SMPS on. This bit informs the internal regulator about external SMPS switch status to decrease regulator output to 0.95 V in Range 2, allowing the external SMPS output down to 1.00 V. Note: This bit is only available on STM32L4P5xx and STM32L4Q5xx devices.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: The external SMPS switch is open

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: The external SMPS switch is closed, internal regulator output is set to 0.95 V

registers.name: SR1
registers.description: Power status register 1

fields.name: WUF1
fields.description: Wakeup flag 1 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on wakeup pin, WKUP1. It is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF1 bit of the PWR_SCR register.

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on wakeup pin, WKUPx

enum_values.name: Cleared
enum_values.description: No wakeup event detected on WKUPx

fields.name: WUF2
fields.description: Wakeup flag 2 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on wakeup pin, WKUP2. It is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF2 bit of the PWR_SCR register.

fields.name: WUF3
fields.description: Wakeup flag 3 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on wakeup pin, WKUP3. It is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF3 bit of the PWR_SCR register.

fields.name: WUF4
fields.description: Wakeup flag 4 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on wakeup pin,WKUP4. It is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF4 bit of the PWR_SCR register.

fields.name: WUF5
fields.description: Wakeup flag 5 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on wakeup pin, WKUP5. It is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF5 bit of the PWR_SCR register.

fields.name: SBF
fields.description: Standby flag This bit is set by hardware when the device enters the Standby mode and is cleared by setting the CSBF bit in the PWR_SCR register, or by a power-on reset. It is not cleared by the system reset.

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: The device did not enter the Standby mode

enum_values.name: Cleared
enum_values.description: The device entered the Standby mode

fields.name: EXT_SMPS_RDY
fields.description: External SMPS ready This bit informs the state of regulator transition from Range 1 to Range 2 Note: This bit is only available on STM32L4P5xx and STM32L4Q5xx devices.

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: Internal regulator not ready in Range 2, the external SMPS cannot be connected

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: Internal regulator ready in Range 2, the external SMPS can be connected

fields.name: WUFI
fields.description: Wakeup flag internal This bit is set when a wakeup is detected on the internal wakeup line. It is cleared when all internal wakeup sources are cleared.

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: This bit is set when a wakeup is detected on the internal wakeup line

enum_values.name: Cleared
enum_values.description: It is cleared when all internal wakeup sources are cleared

registers.name: SR2
registers.description: Power status register 2

fields.name: REGLPS
fields.description: Low-power regulator started This bit provides the information whether the low-power regulator is ready after a power-on reset or a Standby/Shutdown. If the Standby mode is entered while REGLPS bit is still cleared, the wakeup from Standby mode time may be increased.

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: The low-power regulator is not ready

fields.name: REGLPF
fields.description: Low-power regulator flag This bit is set by hardware when the MCU is in Low-power run mode. When the MCU exits from the Low-power run mode, this bit remains at 1 until the regulator is ready in main mode. A polling on this bit must be done before increasing the product frequency. This bit is cleared by hardware when the regulator is ready.

enum_values.name: MR
enum_values.description: The regulator is ready in main mode (MR)

enum_values.name: LPR
enum_values.description: The regulator is in low-power mode (LPR)

fields.name: VOSF
fields.description: Voltage scaling flag A delay is required for the internal regulator to be ready after the voltage scaling has been changed. VOSF indicates that the regulator reached the voltage level defined with VOS bits of the PWR_CR1 register.

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: The regulator is ready in the selected voltage range

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: The regulator output voltage is changing to the required voltage level

fields.name: PVDO
fields.description: Power voltage detector output

enum_values.name: Above
enum_values.description: VDD is above the selected PVD threshold

enum_values.name: Below
enum_values.description: VDD is below the selected PVD threshold

fields.name: PVMO1
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring output: V<sub>DDUSB</sub> vs. 1.2 V Note: PVMO1 is cleared when PVM1 is disabled (PVME1 = 0). After enabling PVM1, the PVM1 output is valid after the PVM1 wakeup time.

enum_values.name: Above
enum_values.description: VDDUSB voltage is above PVM1 threshold (around 1.2 V)

enum_values.name: Below
enum_values.description: VDDUSB voltage is below PVM1 threshold (around 1.2 V)

fields.name: PVMO2
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring output: V<sub>DDIO2</sub> vs. 0.9 V Note: PVMO2 is cleared when PVM2 is disabled (PVME2 = 0). After enabling PVM2, the PVM2 output is valid after the PVM2 wakeup time.

enum_values.name: Above
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 voltage is above PVM2 threshold (around 0.9 V)

enum_values.name: Below
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 voltage is below PVM2 threshold (around 0.9 V)

fields.name: PVMO3
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring output: V<sub>DDA</sub> vs. 1.62 V Note: PVMO3 is cleared when PVM3 is disabled (PVME3 = 0). After enabling PVM3, the PVM3 output is valid after the PVM3 wakeup time.

enum_values.name: Above
enum_values.description: VDDA voltage is above PVM3 threshold (around 1.62 V)

enum_values.name: Below
enum_values.description: VDDA voltage is below PVM3 threshold (around 1.62 V)

fields.name: PVMO4
fields.description: Peripheral voltage monitoring output: V<sub>DDA</sub> vs. 2.2 V Note: PVMO4 is cleared when PVM4 is disabled (PVME4 = 0). After enabling PVM4, the PVM4 output is valid after the PVM4 wakeup time.

enum_values.name: Above
enum_values.description: VDDA voltage is above PVM4 threshold (around 2.2 V)

enum_values.name: Below
enum_values.description: VDDA voltage is below PVM4 threshold (around 2.2 V)

fields.name: CWUF1
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 1 Setting this bit clears the WUF1 flag in the PWR_SR1 register.

enum_values.name: Clear

fields.name: CWUF2

fields.name: CWUF3
